# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm' # Caches npm dependencies

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --prefer-online --no-audit --loglevel=error

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test -- --coverage --testResultsProcessor="jest-junit" # Assuming Jest, add --coverage for coverage reports, and a test results processor if needed

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload build artifacts (optional but good practice)
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: build/ # Adjust path to your build output directory

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-test # This job depends on build-test completing successfully
    environment:
      name: staging
      url: https://staging.example.com # Replace with your staging URL
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download build artifacts (if not building in deploy job)
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: ./build # Adjust path to where you want artifacts downloaded

      - name: Configure AWS credentials (example for AWS S3/EC2/ECS)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: us-east-1 # Replace with your AWS region

      - name: Deploy to S3 (example for static site/frontend)
        run: |
          aws s3 sync ./build s3://your-staging-bucket-name --delete # Adjust source path and S3 bucket name
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id YOUR_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*" # Invalidate CloudFront cache if applicable

      # OR (example for a different deployment method, e.g., SSH to a server)
      # - name: Deploy via SSH
      #   uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.0 # Example SSH action
      #   with:
      #     host: ${{ secrets.STAGING_HOST }}
      #     username: ${{ secrets.STAGING_USERNAME }}
      #     key: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_KEY }}
      #     script: |
      #       # Commands to deploy your application, e.g., pull code, restart service
      #       cd /var/www/your-app-staging
      #       git pull origin main # Or copy build artifacts
      #       npm install --production
      #       pm2 restart your-app # Example for Node.js app with PM2
